title: Data Structure and Algorithm Design Capstone · Algorithm Design Board
summary: Independently specify, implement, trace, test, benchmark, and defend unfamiliar algorithms and data structures under empty, duplicate, adversarial, and changed-scale cases. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.
Choose a computational model and counted operation that matches stakeholder cost.
Distinguish upper, tight, and lower asymptotic bounds without dropping relevant variables.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-dsa algorithm evidence portfolio."""

from collections import deque
from dataclasses import dataclass
from heapq import heappop, heappush
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')

# Add implementation, invariant, counterexample, and measured cost evidence per activity.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Problem Specification correctness-and-cost proof for this exam task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Problem Specification assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Solve a bounded exam instance with 9 inputs. State the invariant, trace each state change, count the chosen operation, and retain the smallest counterexample. Competency probe: Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Problem Specification has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: Define input, output, preconditions, size, and the operation being counted for the exam Problem Specification case
Choose a correct baseline and state its loop or representation invariant for the exam Problem Specification case
Trace the candidate on a minimal instance and derive time and space cost for the exam Problem Specification case
Attack it with boundary and adversarial cases, then justify any optimization for the exam Problem Specification case

steps.title: Choose the regression defense
steps.instruction:
exam case — Solve a bounded exam instance with 5 inputs. State the invariant, trace each state change, count the chosen operation, and retain the smallest counterexample. Competency probe: Defend alternatives, limitations, failure recovery, and measured tradeoffs to reviewers.

A polished result passes one familiar case but fails a boundary case. What is the defensible release decision?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend alternatives, limitations, failure recovery, and measured tradeoffs to reviewers.

content.text: Solve a bounded exam instance with 5 inputs. State the invariant, trace each state change, count the chosen operation, and retain the smallest counterexample. Competency probe: Defend alternatives, limitations, failure recovery, and measured tradeoffs to reviewers.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Design Defense symptom in a defended multi-structure dispatch system during this exam case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Dictionary of Algorithms and Data Structures — Canonical algorithm, complexity, abstract data type, graph, tree, sorting, and search terminology.

stimulus.title: Design Defense failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Fast benchmark results alone justify the design.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend alternatives, limitations, failure recovery, and measured tradeoffs to reviewers.

options.text: Ignore the Design Defense boundary result because the familiar a defended multi-structure dispatch system demonstration passed. This does not resolve choose the regression defense.

Optimize from one happy-path timing, without defining input size, invariant, or an adversarial case for Design Defense. This does not satisfy Design Defense for a defended multi-structure dispatch system.
Stop release, localize the Design Defense cause in a defended multi-structure dispatch system, repair the smallest responsible layer, and rerun the full changed-case set.

steps.title: Submit the certification defense
steps.instruction: Write the exam evidence note for Design Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Solve a bounded exam instance with 6 inputs. State the invariant, trace each state change, count the chosen operation, and retain the smallest counterexample. Competency probe: Defend alternatives, limitations, failure recovery, and measured tradeoffs to reviewers.
steps.why: Retrieving Design Defense through the concrete a defended multi-structure dispatch system case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend alternatives, limitations, failure recovery, and measured tradeoffs to reviewers.
